citations.description: Full content of the portability test file — only two tests present, neither covers client-authored ADR citations

findings.criterion: A client-authored ADR citation backed by a file in the target repository remains eligible as grounded evidence; verified by pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py.
findings.detail: The file tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py contains only two tests: test_operator_attested_coaching_is_self_contained and test_plan_review_prompts_do_not_expose_internal_adr_0043. Neither test exercises a scenario in which a client-authored ADR citation is backed by a file in the target fixture repository and asserts that such a citation is treated as eligible grounded evidence. The entire 34-line test file was read; no such fixture, helper, or assertion exists anywhere in it or in any other test file found by search.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
